name: Risotto Con Pesce Gatto e Erba Cipollina (l A
recipeInstructions:
1. In a pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil with lemon juice, celery, carrot, and fennel. Reduce heat to simmer and add catfish fillets. Cook until just flaking, about 6 to 10 minutes. Remove fish from poaching liquid and reserve the poaching liquid.
2. Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and cook slowly until tender. When onion is tender, add fish and cook for several more minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Let cool.
3. In a separate saute pan, cook wild onions in 1 tbsp butter with a dash of salt and water. Once the water evaporates, add rice and cook until it makes a dry "singing" sound when stirred. Add 1 cup boiling stock and cook until almost all liquid is absorbed by the rice. Repeat with additional cups of stock until rice is creamy and still slightly al dente.
4. Add fish, white wine, and chives to the risotto and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in remaining butter vigorously to create a creamier texture.
5. The risotto should have a slightly soupy consistency when finished. Serve immediately. This recipe makes 4 servings.
Each serving contains about: 648 calories; 260 mg sodium; 131 mg cholesterol; 22 grams fat; 75 grams carbs.; 38 grams protein; 2.6 grams fiber.
